前半の Data Management は、お客様のデータに関する困り事を解決するコンサルティング窓口であり、後半の Landscape Transformation はご相談のみでなく、目指したい姿に向かってお客様のシステムを Transformation する、End to End のソリューションを提供するコンサルティングサービスとなります。

 

連載ラインナップ

Vol.1：M&A とデータマネジメント
M&Aや事業再編において、ビジネス継続と円滑な統合・分離を実現するには何が必要か。Carve-out、Day1対応、TSAなどの典型的な課題を整理します。

Vol.2：Autonomous Enterprise に備えるマスターデータ品質（運用）
自律型エンタープライズの実現に向けて、マスターデータの品質をどう維持・向上させるか。運用の観点から紹介します。

Vol.3：その“データ”、活かすための準備はできていますか？「Data Management Maturity Assessment」とは 
データや AI 活用に向けた、データの大切さ、設計・運用の難しさ、それを打破するきっかけとなる「Data Management Maturity Assessment」についてご紹介します。

Vol.4：Data Health Check と Data Volume Management
データ品質とデータ量の可視化を通じて、システム変革に向けた現状を把握。移行や最適化に向けて押さえるべき分析ポイントを解説します。

Vol.5：Information Lifecycle Management（ILM）
データの保管・アーカイブ・削除を体系的に管理する SAP ILM の全体像と活用方法を紹介します。

Vol.6：Data Privacy (1) — GDPR と PII への対応
欧州 GDPR および個人識別情報（PII）に対するコンプライアンス対応の考え方と、SAP における実践的なアプローチを解説します。

Vol.7：Data Privacy (2) — Masking Solution
開発・テスト環境における個人データ保護として、データマスキングをどう実装・運用するかを紹介します。

Vol.8：大容量システム移行やダウンタイム短縮とデータマネジメント
大容量のデータをより短いダウンタイムで移行する技術とともに、Clean Core 実現とデータボリューム削減を目指すお客様向けのテーラーメイド移行支援をご紹介します。

Vol.9：法規制やビジネス変化に伴う、基幹システムの会計領域を中心としたトランスフォーメーション
SAP 財務会計における主要なデータ構造と、変革プロジェクト時に押さえておくべき管理ポイントを解説します。

 

どなたに読んでいただきたいか

本連載は、特定の製品知識がなくても読み進められるよう、各回でできる限り背景から丁寧に説明します。以下のような方にとって参考になれば幸いです。

  • SAP システムを利用している、またはこれから導入・移行を検討している
  • データマネジメントの重要性は感じているが、何から手を付けてよいか分からない
  • M&A・S/4HANA 移行・クラウド移行などの変革プロジェクトを計画している
  • Business AI Platform でのデータマネジメントが知りたい

各回は独立した内容としてお読みいただけますが、連載全体を通じてデータマネジメントの全体像が見えてくる構成になっています。ぜひ各回の公開をお見逃しなく。

 

次回：Vol.1 - M&Aとデータマネジメント

急なビジネス要求に対し、SAP ERP の観点からどう備えるか。

entry.title: Monitoring of SAP HANA on IBM Power Virtual Servers in IBM Cloud
entry.content:
Introduction

This blog post is about monitoring SAP HANA on IBM Power Virtual Server in IBM Cloud.
As developer in the SAP monitoring part in IBM Cloud I focus on the real features.
Monitoring the health of IT services is a standard.


IBM Cloud offers advanced features on monitoring SAP HANA Databases:

Not only covers the monitoring the recent state and the historical metrics in a timeline,
as early-warning system it allows for measures to prevent failures beforehand.

  •  The dashboard panels can be edited to change or add metric sources and layout
  •  Alarms are available with selfdefined thresholds for all available metrics
  •  Analyse failures in reviewing the historical metrics
  •  Alert views offer statistics of critical selfdefined events within a timeline
  •  Notifications of alert events can be targeted to a wide range of types: email, SMS, pager, Slack notification channel, Microsoft Teams notification channel, Webhooks, OpsGenie

The monitored data allows to

  •  enhance system visibility and control
  •  improve resource utilization and optimization
  •  reduce downtime and increase overall efficiency

Three configuration options are available for monitoring SAP:

  1.  SAP monitoring is enabled with minimal effort as extensions in the catalog tiles 'Power Virtual Server with VPC landing zone' and 'Power Virtual Server for SAP HANA'.
    SAP monitoring is enabled with minimal effort as extensions in the catalog tiles
    Power Virtual Server with VPC landing zone - variation 'Standard Landscape Variation
    and Power Virtual Server for SAP HANA' - variation 'SAP S/4HANA or BW/4HANA
    In the standard version of the infrastructure landscape enable monitoring SAP (via the optional parameter 'enable_monitoring' set to true).
    This triggers the automated creation of an additional VSI for collecting and forwarding SAP metrics,
    and one IBM Cloud sysdig Instance will be created to show SAP metrics in UI Dashboards.
    Readme file - IBM Cloud Solution for Power Virtual Server with VPC Landing Zone Standard Landscape Variation
    The IBM catlog tile 'Power Virtual Server for SAP HANA' offers the input variable 'sap_monitoring_vars'
    to edit the monitoring configuration for the deployable SAP System:
    'config_override': true or false will override existing monitoring configuration numbers
    'sap_monitoring_nr: this number defines the unique monitoring configuration number, visible in the UI dashboard as part of the instance source name
    Readme file - IBM Cloud Catalog - Power Virtual Server for SAP HANA : 'SAP S/4HANA or SAP BW/4HANA

  2. The semi-automated configuration with Ansible roles is described here Monitoring SAP on Power with Ansible roles https://github.com/IBM/ansible-power-linux-sap/blob/main/roles/monitoring_sap/README.md .

  3. The manual configuration of SAP monitoring in IBM Cloud is rather focussing on the technical details and described here Getting started with IBM Cloud Monitoring for SAP systems. 

The metrics of SAP HANA DB are visualized in one dashboard.
Processes and instances are monitored in the SAP System dashbaord.
Up to 99 different SAP Systems can be configured for monitoring on one VSI,
The dashboards show the metrics separated per SAP System or merged into one dashboard.

Summary

IBM Cloud Monitoring for SAP Systems on IBM Power Virtual Server provides tailored dashboards and flexible configuration options to ensure optimal performance and reliability for mission-critical SAP workloads.
With automated, semi-automated, and manual setup choices, organizations can enhance system visibility, optimize resource utilization, and reduce downtime.
Dashboards deliver real-time insights into SAP S/4HANA databases and application metrics, enabling proactive management and improved operational efficiency.

entry.title: From Vision to Transformation: EDP's Strategic SAP Modernization Journey
entry.content:
1. Company Profile

EDP is a leading vertically integrated global energy company headquartered in Lisbon, Portugal, with operations spanning Europe, the Americas, and APAC. Established in 1976, combining power generation, transmission, distribution and retail supply of electricity and natural gas.

EDP’s diversified business is organized principally around three core segments:

  • Renewables — Development, ownership and operation of renewable energy assets including wind (onshore and offshore), solar, hydro and energy storage; administered partly through its major affiliate EDP Renováveis.
  • Networks — Transmission and distribution infrastructure providing reliable delivery of electricity across different markets, with extensive grid assets in key countries.
  • Client Solutions & Energy Management — Customer-facing energy supply, gas commercialization, smart solutions, and energy services for residential, commercial and industrial users.

With a workforce of around 12,000+ employees, EDP serves millions of electricity and gas customers globally and manages a substantial installed generation capacity. It is actively transforming its portfolio toward low-carbon sources, aiming for coal-free operations and a fully renewable mix by 2030, aligned with broader decarbonization and net-zero objectives.

The operating model, includes four business platforms and a set of global business enablement functions, organised in five global regions.

 As SAP Portugal's largest customer, EDP has maintained a strategic partnership with SAP for over 30 years. The company was also among the first organizations to adopt SAP for Utilities (IS-U) in the early 2000s, laying the foundation for a long-standing journey of innovation and digital transformation.

2. Customer Transformation context

Some years ago, EDP defined the future SAP strategy having in consideration the following decision drivers:

  • Instance unification ( Per geography, per capability, per platform)
  • Standardization ( Greenfield, Bluefield, Brownfield)
  • Hosting strategy ( Self Hosted, Mixed, RISE)
  • Integration and interoperability ( SAP proprietary, Mixed, Industry Standards)
  • Development and Change Management ( External microservices, BTP, side by side)

These drivers were classified on the impact and on cost/ROI. In the end, after EDP evaluation, the SAP future strategy for EDP was defined as a strategy with the SAP main pillars: RISE and BTP as part of their reference architecture.

In Q3 2024, their SAP transformation was initiated by signing the 1st RISE contract for the Client Solutions (Utilities Retail B2B and B2C), Q2 2025 an RISE contract for the Networks Spain ( Utilities Distribution) and Q4 2025 an additional RISE contract for their Utilities business in Brazil.

3. The Global Business Services (EDP Shared Services) Transformation

In parallel and as following stage for their transformation, EDP was evaluating the remaining business platforms and defined as part of the strategy to evolve the Global Business Services (GBS) platform by 2028. The GBS platform includes 2 different scopes and systems: SIM-F for Finance & Logistics and SIM-H for HR payroll processes (EDP has the majority of the SuccessFactors solutions).

 The GBS platform has the responsibility to EDP has a shared services company and supports the 1500 EDP’s companies in 24 countries.

In early 2023, SAP initiated a collaborative study by a VIS service (by the MaxAttention team) to assess possible paths for EDP to evolve the SIM-F that is currently in SAP ECC. That service allowed EDP to understand all the possible paths to evolve to S/4 and SAP recommendations on different dimensions such as migration, integration, custom code and best practices. The assessment was further strengthened by a comprehensive business case, developed collaboratively with EDP and supported by SAP Value Advisory, to quantify the expected business value and establish a robust foundation for decision-making.

EDP objective for this evolution was set clearly has “The move to SAP S/4HANA enables EDP to transform business processes through Fit to Standard, Clean Core and Efficiency while enabling innovation at scale for the future. As part of this initiative, EDP initiated an RFP to enable a global negotiation with SAP, encompassing the expanded EDP GBS scope, as well as the consolidation of SuccessFactors licensing, SAP services, SAP AI, SAP BTP and SAP BDC negotiations.

 The global negotiations were finalized and signed at the end of Q2 2026, enabling EDP to initiate the GBS project named EDGE.

4. Future Direction of EDP and the SAP Transformation Program

EDP's SAP transformation journey does not end here. As part of the broader EDP Global Business Services (GBS) platform strategy, EDP will continue to assess the adoption of SAP Central Finance and SAP Group Reporting to further enhance and standardize its financial management and consolidation processes.

 Looking ahead, the organization will evaluate another major transformation initiative: the modernization of its largest SAP landscape supporting electricity distribution operations in Portugal, including SAP for Utilities and Enterprise Asset Management solutions. This strategic program will be a key priority throughout 2026 and 2027, with activities focused on defining the transformation roadmap and preparing the Request for Proposal, which is expected to be launched in late 2027 or early 2028.

While significant milestones have already been achieved, this marks the beginning rather than the end of EDP's transformation journey. With a clear vision, strong executive sponsorship, and a commitment to continuous innovation.

entry.title: How to Connect Claude Code to ABAP with Zero-Config MCP
entry.content:
Before We Start - What This Post Deliberately Skips
- What is Claude Code, and what are the available models?
- What is MCP (Model Context Protocol) and how it works conceptually?
- What is vibe coding and why AI-assisted development matters?
- What are the benefits of using AI in SAP development?
- What development platforms are available (Web, Desktop, CLI)?
If you need more details, you can find these topics in sap community portal. Read that, then come back here.

What This Post Covers - and Why It Matters?

This post walks you through connecting Claude Code to a live SAP system using the ABAP Remote Filesystem extension's native MCP server - no external Node.js setup, no manual JSON editing, just a single toggle. 

What this changes for SAP development:

  • No more hand-typed routine code. TYPE declarations, SELECT statements, ALV setup - written directly into the SAP object from a single prompt.
  • No more AI guesswork. Without a live connection, AI tools hallucinate tables and function modules that don't exist. With MCP, every suggestion is grounded in your actual system.
  • No more setup overhead. What used to require a custom Node.js project now takes one checkbox and one command - open to any developer with VS Code.
  • Clean Core by default. Claude works through the same ADT API and released objects that Eclipse uses, so generated code fits your governance model instead of fighting it.

This is the point where AI-assisted ABAP stops being a chat-window experiment and starts being a real part of your IDE.

 
Goal: I will cover the entire topic in 4 parts:
|Part 1| Installing Claude Code on Windows and setting up the VS Code extension |
|Part 2| Connecting VS Code to your live SAP system via the ABAP FS extension |
|Part 3| Enabling the native MCP server with a single toggle - no Node.js setup, no JSON editing |
|Part 4| A live demo: one plain-English prompt creates a real, running ABAP report on SAP |
If you're here, you already know why this matters. Let's get to the actual steps.
 
Pre-requisite: This guide assumes you already have an active Claude subscription (Pro, Max, Team, or Enterprise). If not, sign up at claude.ai before proceeding
 
Part 1 - The Setup: Getting Claude Code Running on Windows
Step 1.1 - Install Claude Code CLI
Open PowerShell (the built-in Windows command-line shell) and run:

 

This downloads Anthropic's official installer and executes it in one step. 'irm' fetches the script, 'iex' runs it.
> ⚠️ Common Error - Git Bash Not Found
> Claude Code needs a bash shell to run on Windows. If you see this error, install Git for Windows from [git-scm.com/downloads/win](https://git-scm.com/downloads/win). During setup, select: Git from the command line and also from 3rd-party software. Then re-run the install command above.
Step 1.2 — Verify the Installation

 

Close PowerShell and open a fresh window. Run:
You should see a version number like 'claude 1.x.x'. If PowerShell says claude is not recognized, the PATH wasn't set automatically. Fix it manually:

 

1. Start → search Environment Variables in system settings
2. Edit the environment variables for your account
3. Select Path → Edit → New → paste ( in this case path can be taken from powershell commnad prompt)
 for eg: 

 

4. Close and reopen PowerShell, then re-run claude --version.

> Run claude doctor - this command checks your PATH, authentication, Node.js version, and config in one shot. Always the first step when something isn't working.
Step 1.3 - Launch Claude Code and the VS Code Extension
Navigate to your project folder and launch Claude Code.
The first launch opens your browser to authenticate with your Anthropic account (Pro, Max, Team, or Enterprise all work). 
For everyday ABAP work, the VS Code extension is where you'll spend most of your time.
Download and Install VS code from code.visualstudio.com for windows if you don't already have it. Once it is installed.Open VS Code, press 'Ctrl+Shift+X', search for Claude Code (published by Anthropic), and install it. Once installed, the Claude icon appears in the Activity Bar and connects automatically to your account.

 

The extension gives you a persistent chat panel inside VS Code - ask Claude about the file you have open, highlight any ABAP block and ask it to explain or refactor, and switch between ABAP and CAP projects in the same session. No terminal switching required.
> Note on Software Center installs: If your organization restricts installations, Claude Code for VS Code is typically available via your company Software Center / Company Portal.
 
Part 2 - Connecting VS Code to Your SAP System via ABAP FS
This is where the magic begins. The ABAP Remote Filesystem (ABAP FS)extension - built by Marcello Urbani - connects VS Code to your SAP system using the same ADT REST API that Eclipse uses. Once connected, your ABAP objects (programs, classes, CDS views, behavior definitions) appear as files in VS Code. Claude Code can then read, write, and review them like any local code file.
Pre-requisites Before You Connect
You'll need these four things from your SAP system before starting:
| Hostname or IP | The address of your SAP server (hostname preferred - IP causes SSL issues explained below) |
| ADT Service active | Go to transaction SICF → '/sap/bc/adt' must be activated |
| Client number| e.g. '100' - visible in the SAP logon screen |
|Username & Password| Your regular SAP development user |
| Port | HTTPS: '44300' (typical) | HTTP: '8000' for instance 00, '8001' for instance 01 |
 
Step 2.1 - Install and Configure ABAP FS
1. In VS Code Extensions ('Ctrl+Shift+X'), search ABAP remote filesystem by Marcello Urbani → Install.
2. Press 'Ctrl+Shift+P' → type ABAP FS: Connection Manager → open it.
3. Add a new connection and fill in the fields:
   - Name: anything you like (e.g. DEV or SANDBOX)
   - Server URL:'https://<hostname>:44300'
   - ⚠️ Do NOT add '/sap/bc/adt' to the URL - the extension adds this automatically. If you include it, the path doubles and the connection fails.
   - Client, Username, Language: 'EN'
4. Save.
5. Press 'Ctrl+Shift+P' → ABAP FS: Connect to an SAP system→ select your connection → enter password.
 

 

> ⚠️ SSL Certificate Error (very common on internal systems)
> If you see IP does not match certificate's altnames - this is because your SAP system's SSL certificate was issued with a hostname, not the IP. The fix: Get the hostname (e.g. 'https://sapdev.company.com:44300') and use that instead of the IP. 
> ⚠️ADT Service Not Found
> If you see Resource /sap/bc/adt does not exist - the ADT service isn't activated. Run tcode SICF → navigate to default_host → 'sap/bc/adt' → right-click → Activate Service.
 
Step 2.2 - Navigating ABAP Objects
ABAP FS gives you two ways to find objects. Object Search lets you find anything by name instantly. If you prefer a tree view like Eclipse ADT, enable SAP GUI Integration in your connection settings - this gives you a full package tree (Dictionary, Source Code Library, Transactions) and even embeds SAP GUI for HTML directly inside VS Code, so you can run transactions without leaving the editor.
1. Click OBJECT SEARCH in the left sidebar
2. Type the object or package name (e.g. 'ZCL_MY_CLASS' or 'Z_MY_PACKAGE')
3. Double-click any result to open it as a text file in VS Code
4. To edit: right-click  → edit → save (changes push back to SAP automatically)
Once an object is open in VS Code, open the integrated terminal (Ctrl+) and run claude. Some prompts that immediately pay off:

 

Part 3 -The Game Changer: Enabling the Native MCP Server

 

This is the step that changes everything - and the reason this post exists.
Why ABAP FS Alone Isn't Enough?
With only ABAP FS connected, Claude can see only the one file you currently have open. Ask it to create a new class and it might invent table names, function modules, or BAdI definitions that don't exist on your system. It's helpful, but it's guessing.
          ABAP FS Only                                         ABAP FS + MCP
Claude sees what we search Manually Claude autonomously searches any object on the system
Hallucinates objects it hasn't seen Grounded in live SAP system data
You copy-paste code manually Claude reads, writes, activates autonomously
No transport awareness Creates & assigns transports via ADT

 

The MCP (Model Context Protocol) server gives Claude Code a live connection to your SAP system's ADT API - the same backend Eclipse uses. Claude stops guessing and starts knowing.
Step 3.1 - Enable the MCP Server (One Toggle)
This is where the zero-config part comes in. You don't need to install anything extra, clone a Node.js repo, or edit JSON files manually.
Press 'Ctrl+Shift+P' → search ABAP FS MCP → select it → enable Autostart.
That's it. The ABAP FS extension spins up a local MCP server on your machine (default port 4847). You'll see a VS Code notification confirming it's running:
> ABAP MCP Server running on port 4847. External AI clients can connect to http://localhost:4847/mcp

Step 3.2 - Register the MCP Server with Claude Code
Now tell Claude Code where to find the ABAP tools. Run this in the VS Code integrated terminal:
This registers the local MCP server with Claude Code. The configuration is saved to your '.claude.json' file and persists across sessions - you only need to do this once.
Step 3.3 - Verify the Connection

 

open the terminal and run this command once the MCP server is added 
You should see:
Claude Code will confirm when the server is registered:
> abap-remote-fs has been added with HTTP transport at http://localhost:4847/mcp"*
The Complete Architecture
Claude Code can now autonomously:
- Search and read any ABAP object on the live SAP system
- write → syntax check → activate → unlock objects
- Create transport requests and assign objects
- Run ATC checks and read short dumps - all from a plain-English prompt
> Golden - In Auto mode, Claude will write, syntax check, and activate without pausing - that's the power of the agentic workflow. If you want to review before anything is activated, switch to Plan mode  shift+tab to cycle modes)  Claude will show you the full plan and wait for your approval. For production-adjacent systems, always run in Plan or Step mode.
Part 4 - Live Demo: One Prompt, One Real ABAP Program on SAP
Disclaimer: This is my first time setting up all this and explore, so i just started with a very simple scenario. Going forward will create RAP objects and other complex artifacts and see the efficiency.
Let me show you what all of this enables. Here's the exact prompt I used:
> Create a new ABAP executable program ZCL_DEMO_REPORT_01 in my ZVS_DEMO package. Fetch data from MARA for a user-specified Material Number (select-option) and display Material Number, Type, and Created By in an ALV grid. Write in ABAP 7.5 syntax.
One prompt. No templates. No copy-paste. Here's what Claude did.
Act 1 - Claude Plans Before It Codes
Claude didn't immediately start writing code. It:
1. Confirmed the target package ('ZVS_DEMO') exists on the system
2. Detected that the program creation triggered a **transport request dialog** — and recognised it was the likely source of a cancellation
3. Called the SAP ADT API via Bash to **list existing open transports**
4. Selected the correct transport and retried the creation
There was a little bit delay on my end to confirm the transport. and it gave me this message.
> Package confirmed. The cancellation is likely a transport request dialog. Let me check for existing transports, then retry.
This is the part that impresses ABAP developers most. Claude handled a real SAP workflow quirk .
Act 2 - The Program Appears on SAP
Claude then created the program on the live system. You can watch its autonomous todo list mid-execution:
- Create ABAP program ZCL_DEMO_REPORT_01 in ZVS_DEMO package via MCP
- Get workspace URI for the new program
- Write ABAP source code into the program
- Activate the program and check diagnostics
'ZCL_DEMO_REPORT_01' appeared in the VS Code Explorer sidebar - a real, registered ABAP program on the SAP system, not a local file.
You can see initially there was no program created in my package 
After few seconds, the program is created 

 

Act 3 - Full ABAP 7.5 Source Code Written
With the object created, Claude fetched the workspace URI and wrote the complete source code directly into the SAP program. What it generated:
No placeholder code. Real, working ABAP in the correct syntax release, referencing actual MARA field types from the live system.
Act 4 - Running the Program
After activation, there are two ways to run it:
Option A - Classic SAP GUI:Log in to SAP GUI →run the program → Execute
Option B - Embedded SAP Web GUI(what I used): VS Code can open the SAP Web GUI directly in a browser tab, embedded inside the IDE. No separate login or window switching required.
Enter selection criteria:
The selection screen appeared with the Matnr  field exactly as specified. I entered a range of 11to 21. The value help (F4) shows the available materials in the system
Material Number, Material Type, and Created By - exactly what was asked for. Live data from MARA. Report running.
 
Summary:
|Claude understands SAP context| It identified the transport dialog, queried existing transports via ADT, and handled a real SAP workflow quirk autonomously 
| It created a real SAP object| 'ZCL_DEMO_REPORT_01' exists on the live system, created via ADT REST API through MCP - not a file on disk 
| Production-quality ABAP 7.5 | TYPE declarations referencing actual MARA field types, correct naming conventions, modern SQL syntax 
| Minutes, not hours| The developer's job shifts to reviewing - not writing skeletons, hunting transport dialogs, or looking up field types 
| Fully iterative| Once created, you can ask Claude to add authority checks, error handling, ALV field catalogues, or additional filters - each iteration in seconds 
| You stay in control | Claude's todo list is transparent - you see every step it plans. You review the code. You decide what activates 
 
Where to Go from Here?
Here are five things to try once you have the setup working:
1. Understand inherited code - Explain the purpose and flow of report Z_*_REPORT. Include tables accessed, authority checks, deprecated patterns like SELECT .
2. Generate a CDS view - Create a CDS view hierarchy for Sales Order analysis: interface view joining VBAK+VBAP, projection view with Fiori Elements. UI annotations for a List Report.
3. Build a Travel Request POC -  RAP Business Object with CRUD, approve/reject action, draft handling, OData V4 binding, Fiori Elements UI.
4. Write ABAP Unit tests - Generate a unit test class 
5. Code review and modernization - Review this class for Clean ABAP violations, missing authority checks, SELECT * usage, and SELECT inside LOOP. Propose S/4-equivalent replacements.
 
The bottom line: The integration that used to require manual Node.js setup, JSON editing, and external package management now takes a single Ctrl+Shift+P toggle and one terminal command.
 
The ABAP Remote Filesystem team has done the hard work. Claude Code does the coding. You do the review.
Give it a try - and let me know in the comments what use case you tackle first.
 
A personal note  : This is my first time diving deeper into vibe coding and MCP concepts. I come from a classic ABAP background, and everything covered here -  from the VS Code setup to the MCP server to the live demo - I explored and built from scratch over the past few weeks. The scenario is intentionally simple. The goal was to get the fundamentals right, document them honestly, and share what works. There is a lot more ground to cover - RAP generation at scale, Clean Core extensibility patterns, ATC pre-checks via Claude Code, and deeper agentic workflows. I'll be going further in the posts ahead. If you're also just getting started with this, I hope this gives you a solid, working foundation to build on. And if you're already further ahead - I'd love to hear what you've learnt in the comments.
 
Note: All screenshots in this post are taken from a live S/4HANA development system. Code generated by Claude Code should always be reviewed and tested in a sandbox environment before activation in any productive system.

entry.title: SAP Fiori Tools 2607 Update: Skills, Fullstack AI Development, and Performance
entry.content:
The 2607 release of SAP Fiori tools focuses on AI-driven development, but there is also good news for the human tooling: faster startup, more Page Editor capabilities, and a few quality-of-life fixes. Here is what is new.

AI-Driven Development with the MCP Server for SAP Fiori

MCP support in SAP Business Application Studio

SAP Business Application Studio now includes an AI Chat capability that you can use for MCP-supported AI-driven SAP Fiori development directly from the browser. No installation is needed: the MCP server for SAP Fiori comes pre-installed in SAP Fiori and Fullstack Dev spaces, alongside the UI5 and CAP MCP servers.

Installing the MCP server locally

For local development, the MCP server for SAP Fiori is now available through the MCP registry on GitHub. You can also install it as a Claude plugin, together with our new skills (more on that below).

By the way, if you have the SAP Fiori tools extension pack installed in VS Code, you automatically have a version of the MCP server for SAP Fiori available that can be consumed by the GitHub Copilot extension.

Skills: the next layer on top of MCP

MCP servers provide the tools an AI agent can call, but tools alone do not tell the agent how to approach a complex, multi-step development job. That is the job of a skill. A skill bundles domain knowledge, step-by-step guidance, and best practices into a reusable prompt-based workflow that directs the AI through a specific task. Instead of leaving the model to figure out the right sequence of tool calls on its own, a skill encodes that knowledge explicitly, leading to more reliable and consistent results.

With this release, the SAP Fiori tools team publishes the first set of skills that complement the MCP server for SAP Fiori:

SkillWhat it does
sap-fiori-app-developmentGuides the creation and modification of SAP Fiori elements applications in CAP and standalone projects
sap-fiori-analytical-chartAdds an analytical chart and table hybrid to a List Report, supporting CAP and ABAP RAP (OData V4)
sap-fiori-add-visual-filterAdds chart-based visual filters to the filter bar or value help in SAP Fiori elements applications
sap-fiori-create-cliRuns and automates the @​sap-ux/create CLI for generating, converting, and managing Fiori projects
sap-fiori-eslint-pluginConfigures, migrates, or runs ESLint with the @​sap-ux/eslint-plugin-fiori-tools in SAP Fiori projects

The easiest way to get the skills is through the Claude plugin, where they are included alongside the MCP server for SAP Fiori. Alternatively, you can install individual skills via skills.sh, an open ecosystem for reusable agent skills that supports Cursor, GitHub Copilot, and others.

We will continuously expand and improve this set of skills, and we are very much looking forward to your feedback.

Fullstack development in VS Code

The MCP server for SAP Fiori pairs with the new ABAP MCP server, which is part of the ABAP development tools for Visual Studio Code. Together, the two bridge the gap between backend ABAP services and the SAP Fiori frontend, enabling a true fullstack AI-driven development experience from within VS Code. For a concrete walkthrough of how this can look in practice, I recommend watching my ABAPConf presentation.

A new top-level tool structure

Based on feedback we have collected from usage, we restructured the top-level tools of the MCP server for SAP Fiori. More capabilities are now surfaced directly at the top level, in particular the app generators and metadata retrieval, which are needed to properly connect backend services to the frontend layer. This restructuring is available as of version 1.8.1 of the MCP server for SAP Fiori.

The top-level tools now are:

ToolWhat it does
search_docsSearches SAP Fiori elements, annotations, UI5, OPA5, and SAP Fiori tools documentation
list_fiori_appsScans a directory to find existing SAP Fiori applications
list_sap_systemsLists SAP systems stored in the user's environment
download_odata_service_metadataDownloads the EDMX metadata of an OData service from an SAP system
generate_fiori_app_odataGenerates a new SAP Fiori application for OData (non-CAP) projects, for example RAP-based services
generate_fiori_app_capGenerates a new SAP Fiori application inside an existing CAP project
list_functionality (Step 1 of 3)Lists supported modification operations for an existing SAP Fiori application
get_functionality_details (Step 2 of 3)Returns the required parameters for a chosen modification
execute_functionality (Step 3 of 3)Executes the modification with the provided parameters

This structure also sets the stage for what comes next. We are working on adaptation project support for the MCP server for SAP Fiori, which will add tools for creating and modifying adaptation projects directly through AI agents. They will join the top-level structure shown above.

Renovations and Performance Improvements

Moving from CommonJS to ECMAScript Modules

ECMAScript Modules (ESM) are the official JavaScript module standard, supported natively in modern browsers and Node.js. With this release, the SAP Fiori tools suite and its Node.js modules migrate from CommonJS to ESM. For us, this means better ecosystem compatibility, tree-shaking for smaller bundles (unused code eliminated at build time), and more efficient asynchronous module loading.

ESLint 10 upgrade

The 2603 release delivered ESLint 9 support for the tools and generator. With 2607 we take the next step and upgrade to ESLint 10, keeping the toolchain current and continuing the move toward the modern flat config format.

80% faster extension startup

As the SAP Fiori tools extensions have grown over time, startup time had crept up correspondingly. Through targeted refactoring and more efficient bundling, loading only what is necessary at startup, we reduced the startup time of the SAP Fiori tools extensions by around 80%.

Other Improvements

A few smaller additions that are worth calling out:

  • Fiori Generator error details: Clicking on an error in the generator now opens the full error details in the console, making it much easier to diagnose what went wrong.
  • Page Editor building blocks: The Page Editor now supports Page, Form, and Field building blocks in custom pages and custom sections, rounding out the building block coverage for custom development.
  • SAP Fiori elements enhancements via Page Editor: The Page Editor now lets you configure a notes reuse component, inline edit, mass edit, and creation dialogs (and more) for SAP Fiori elements applications, all through the graphical configuration interface without touching annotations manually.
  • Reuse existing LineItem annotations for tables: When adding a table, you can now select an existing LineItem annotation from the service instead of always having to create a new one. This avoids duplicate annotations and makes it easier to build UIs on top of well-annotated services.
  • Unique control IDs in XML generation: The Page Editor now correctly generates unique control IDs when producing XML for custom elements.
  • Download UI5 ABAP repository content from the public cloud: You can now download source artifacts directly from a deployed SAPUI5 ABAP repository, so you can continue local development even when the source files are not available. This closes a gap for public cloud customers, who previously had no way to import from the ABAP backend. See the SAP Help Portal documentation for more information.

Staying Up to Date

If you want to track changes in SAP Fiori tools between release blog posts, the What's New section in the SAP Help Portal is the best place to look. It is updated continuously with newly delivered capabilities and fixes.

For documentation and sample code covering all building blocks available when creating SAP Fiori elements applications, visit the SAP Fiori development portal.

Questions and feedback? Leave a comment below or reach out through the community.

entry.title: SAP Green Ledger: Q2-26 Release Updates and Highlights
entry.content:
Welcome to the Q2 2026 edition of our SAP Green Ledger Quarterly Release Highlights! This blog summarizes the most important SAP Green Ledger innovations and enhancements delivered during Q2 2026. Highlights include new capabilities for carbon traceability, carbon collection processing, Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM), carbon allocations, analytics, and usability improvements across SAP Green Ledger.

Read on to learn more about the key innovations introduced throughout Q2 2026:

  • Carbon Traceability with Related SAP S/4HANA Documents
  • Mass Reversal of Carbon Collections
  • Simplified CBAM Processing
  • Flexible Carbon Allocations
  • Analytical Dashboard Update
  • Further Updates and Enhancements

 

Carbon Traceability with Related SAP S/4HANA Documents

The Manage Carbon Collections app now includes a new "Related Documents" section that allows you to maintain and view references to the SAP S/4HANA documents from which emissions originate. Supported reference types include:

  • Purchasing Documents
  • Accounting Documents
  • Other Documents

If you import material movements into SAP Green Ledger from a connected SAP S/4HANA system, related document references are transferred automatically. When importing carbon data via CSV files, you can also include related document information in the file. For manually created carbon collections, you can maintain related documents directly in the Manage Carbon Collections app. These references are then carried through from the carbon collection into the corresponding journal entries in the Manage Journal Entries app. This allows you to trace carbon postings throughout the accounting process, from the originating business documents to the resulting carbon journal entries, also improving auditability of carbon data.

Related Documents in a Carbon CollectionTo further simplify navigation and reporting, you can now also filter in the Manage Carbon Collections, Manage Journal Entries and Display Line Items apps by Related Document Type and Related Document ID. This helps you quickly locate carbon records for specific business transactions. For more details check SAP Help: Create a Carbon Collection and Use a CSV Template.

 

Mass Reversal of Carbon Collections

The Manage Carbon Collections app now supports reversing multiple posted carbon collections in a single step. Instead of reversing each carbon collection individually, you can now select and reverse multiple posted carbon collections at once. This significantly reduces manual effort, particularly after correcting mass uploads. Up to 5,000 carbon collections can be reversed in a single operation. For more information, see SAP Help: Post a Carbon Collection to the Journal.

 

Simplified CBAM Processing

Several enhancements improve the processing and monitoring of Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) carbon collections while also increasing transparency for CBAM accounts configuration.

Efficient Processing of CBAM Carbon Collections

The Manage Carbon Collections app now also supports reversing CBAM carbon collections. In addition, you can now post and reverse multiple CBAM carbon collections in a single step. As a result, you can process large volumes of carbon collections that require carbon certificates more efficiently.

When reversing CBAM carbon collections, the resulting journal entries are created with the journal entry source type Carbon Collection Reversal, providing a clear audit trail for correction postings. When posting or reversing multiple CBAM carbon collections, SAP Green Ledger automatically calculates a single fair value per company code. This reduces manual effort and ensures consistent valuation across company codes.

To support the monitoring of these postings, the Manage Carbon Liabilities app now supports filtering carbon liabilities by Posted and Reversed status. With that, you can quickly monitor the processing status of carbon liabilities and identify relevant entries. Check out SAP Help for more details: Post a Carbon Collection to the Journal, Fair Value Calculation, and View Carbon Liabilities.

Filter for Posted and Reversed Carbon Liabilities in the Manage Carbon Liabilities App

Improved Visibility into EU CBAM Accounts by Company Code

The Configure Carbon Certificate Data app now displays the EU CBAM accounts maintained in the Determine Mandatory Accounts app for each company code. This allows you to view the configured account assignments directly while working with carbon certificate data, without switching between applications. See also SAP Help: Configure Carbon Certificate Data and Determine Mandatory Accounts.

 EU CBAM Accounts per Company Code in Configure Carbon Certificate Data App

 

Flexible Carbon Allocations

Several enhancements make carbon allocations more flexible and align them more closely with financial accounting. Our new capabilities can help you reuse existing allocation logic and support project-based allocation scenarios.

Copy Financial Flow for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

The ability to create allocation models by copying financial flows in the Manage Allocations app is now also available for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. Previously available only for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, this enhancement enables you to create carbon allocation models based on the allocation patterns already used in your connected SAP S/4HANA system.

Allocation Model Copied from Financial Flow

Using the latest Cost Center Accounting data from the connected SAP S/4HANA system, SAP Green Ledger automatically copies the receiving objects and allocation percentages and transfers them into the allocation model. This eliminates the need to manually maintain allocation rules and helps keep carbon allocations aligned with financial allocations. For more details, check SAP Help: Create Allocation Models by Copying from Financial Flow.

Project-Level Carbon Allocations with WBS Elements

The Manage Allocations app now supports Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) elements as both sender and receiver dimensions when creating periodic allocation models. For both the sender and receiver, you can now choose a WBS element or a cost center as the allocation dimension, enabling allocation scenarios such as WBS-to-WBS, WBS-to-Cost Center, Cost Center-to-WBS, and Cost Center-to-Cost Center. To ensure consistency with the existing allocation behavior for cost centers, the corresponding Profit Center and Segment are now automatically derived as well when a WBS element is used as the receiver dimension.

WBS Elements Used as Sender and Receiver Dimensions in a Periodic Allocation Model

 To further improve transparency, Partner WBS Elements are now included in journal entry line items and displayed in both the Manage Journal Entries and Display Line Items apps. You can also filter, group, and sort by Partner WBS Element in the Display Line Items app, giving you better visibility into carbon allocation flows across project structures. For more details, see Create Period Allocation Models.

 

Analytical Dashboard Update

The SAP Green Ledger Analytical Dashboard has also been updated for Q2 2026 with the latest analytical capabilities for combined financial and carbon reporting. For a detailed overview of these enhancements, see the dedicated SAP Community blog post and the SAP Help Portal topic Analyze Carbon Data Alongside Financial Data.

 

Further Updates and Enhancements

Several smaller enhancements further improve the usability of SAP Green Ledger.

Improved Journal Entry Usability

The Manage Journal Entries app now allows you to maximize the Line Items table. You can review journal entries with many line items more comfortably and reduce the need for scrolling. Additionally, each line item is now assigned a stable unique identifier that remains unchanged when the table is sorted or filtered. The stable identifier makes it easier to reference individual line items and improves navigation during journal analysis.

Updated Posting Terminology

The posting action in the Manage Carbon Collections app has been renamed from Post Carbon Collection to simply Post. The related posting statuses are:

  • Posting
  • Posted
  • Posting Failed
New Origin Business System Filter

A new Origin Business System filter is now available in the Manage Journal Entries, Display Line Items, and Display Account Balances apps. Selecting an origin business system automatically limits the available master data to values from that system. Likewise, when a master data object that is linked to a specific business system, such as a company code, is selected, the corresponding business system is identified automatically and applied to the remaining filters.

 

Additional Information

If you want to dive deeper into SAP Green Ledger, the SAP Help Portal is a good place to start. It provides detailed information on concepts, configuration, and application usage. You can also check the What’s New section, which is updated with every release and gives a complete overview of the latest additions. To see what's coming next, the SAP Road Map Explorer offers insight into planned features and upcoming innovations. It is updated regularly and outlines how the solution will continue to evolve.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA OTC – Automating Minimum Order Surcharge Using AMIW/AMIZ + MV45AFZZ Enhancement
entry.content:
A Real Client Implementation (2023)

In 2023, I implemented a pricing enhancement for a client running SAP S/4HANA in the Order-to-Cash (OTC) area. The business wanted to enforce a minimum order value policy and automatically apply a surcharge for orders below a threshold of 5,000 USD.

However, the challenge was that not all customers should be charged. Large retailers such as Walmart regularly place high-volume orders and must be exempt.

This blog explains:

  • The business requirement

  • Standard SAP capabilities (AMIW/AMIZ)

  • Why standard configuration was insufficient

  • The enhancement approach using MV45AFZZ

  • ABAP code snippet

  • Configuration steps 

  • Final solution architecture

📌 Business Requirement

Minimum Order Value Policy

  • Threshold: 5,000 USD

  • If order value < 5,000 USD → Apply surcharge

  • Surcharge must be automatically proposed in the sales order

  • Certain customers must be excluded (e.g., Walmart)

Technical Requirements

  • Logic must run during sales order save

  • Surcharge must be applied using AMIZ condition type

  • Threshold check must use AMIW

  • Customer-specific exemption list must be configurable

  • No manual intervention by sales users

🧩 Standard SAP Configuration: AMIW & AMIZ

SAP provides two condition types for minimum order logic:

AMIW – Minimum Order Value Check

Used to validate whether the order meets the minimum value.

AMIZ – Minimum Order Surcharge

Used to apply a surcharge when AMIW fails.

Configuration Steps

1️⃣ Define Condition Types

  • AMIW – Minimum Order Value

  • AMIZ – Minimum Order Surcharge

SPRO → Sales and Distribution → Basic Functions → Pricing → Pricing Control → Define Condition Types

 

2️⃣ Create Access Sequence

The client required multiple criteria (customer, sales org, material group), so we created a custom access sequence.

V/07 – Access Sequence ZAMIW

 

3️⃣ Maintain Condition Records

  • AMIW → 5,000 USD

  • AMIZ → surcharge amount (e.g., 150 USD)

VK11 – AMIW condition record VK11 – AMIZ condition record

 

❗ Why Standard Logic Was Not Enough

SAP standard AMIW → AMIZ flow does not support:

  • Customer-specific exemptions

  • Conditional surcharge logic

  • Triggering surcharge only at save

  • Complex business rules based on order value + customer category

Therefore, we enhanced the logic using MV45AFZZ.

 

🔧 Enhancement: MV45AFZZ (USEREXIT_SAVE_DOCUMENT)

We implemented the logic in:

MV45AFZZ → USEREXIT_SAVE_DOCUMENT

This exit runs during sales order save, ensuring final pricing and order value are validated.

 

🔍 Enhancement Logic Overview

✔ Steps Performed in the Exit

  1. Read customer number (KUNNR)

  2. Check if customer is exempt

  3. Calculate total order value (net value)

  4. Compare with 5,000 USD threshold

  5. If below threshold → read AMIZ condition record

  6. Insert surcharge condition into order pricing

  7. Update XVBAP/XKOMV tables

🧑‍💻 ABAP Code Snippet (MV45AFZZ)

Below is a simplified version of the enhancement logic:

 
 
USEREXIT_SAVE_DOCUMENT.

DATA: lv_kunnr       TYPE kunnr,
      lv_order_value TYPE netwr,
      lv_threshold   TYPE netwr VALUE '5000',
      lv_surcharge   TYPE kbetr,
      lt_exempt      TYPE TABLE OF kunnr,
      ls_pricing     TYPE komv.

"1. Read customer from order header
lv_kunnr = xvbpa-kunnr.

"2. Maintain exemption list (could also be a Z-table)
lt_exempt = VALUE #( ( '0000100001' )  "Walmart
                     ( '0000100002' )  "Costco
                   ).

IF lv_kunnr IN lt_exempt.
  RETURN. "Customer exempt from surcharge
ENDIF.

"3. Calculate order value
lv_order_value = xvbap-netwr.

"4. Compare with threshold
IF lv_order_value < lv_threshold.

  "5. Read AMIZ condition record
  CALL FUNCTION 'RV_CONDITION_READ'
    EXPORTING
      condition_type = 'AMIZ'
    IMPORTING
      kbetr          = lv_surcharge.

  "6. Insert surcharge into pricing
  CLEAR ls_pricing.
  ls_pricing-kschl = 'AMIZ'.
  ls_pricing-kbetr = lv_surcharge.
  ls_pricing-waers = 'USD'.
  APPEND ls_pricing TO xkomv.

ENDIF.

✔ Notes

  • In a real implementation, the exemption list is stored in a Z-table

  • Pricing update uses XKOMV

  • Order value calculation may include header-level logic

  • Error handling and logging added in production version

 

🔄 Solution Architecture (Text Diagram)

 
 
                +---------------------------+
                |   Sales Order Creation    |
                +---------------------------+
                             |
                             v
                +---------------------------+
                |   Standard Pricing (AMIW) |
                +---------------------------+
                             |
                | Order < 5000? |
                             |
                 Yes ------------------ No
                 |                      |
                 v                      v
      +-------------------+     +-------------------+
      | MV45AFZZ Exit     |     | Save Order        |
      | Check Exemption   |     +-------------------+
      | Apply AMIZ        |
      +-------------------+
                 |
                 v
      +---------------------------+
      | Surcharge Added to Order |
      +---------------------------+
 

📈 Business Benefits

  • Fully automated surcharge logic

  • Zero manual pricing adjustments

  • Customer-specific flexibility

  • Accurate enforcement of minimum order policy

  • Clean audit trail

  • Reusable logic for other pricing scenarios

 

🏁 Final Thoughts

This enhancement showcases how SAP S/4HANA OTC allows us to combine:

  • Standard pricing capabilities (AMIW/AMIZ)

  • Custom access sequences

  • Targeted ABAP enhancements (MV45AFZZ)

…to deliver a solution that is both flexible and business-driven.

#SAP #SAPECC #SAPSD #SAPABAP #SAPPricing #SAPEnhancement #OrderToCash #OTC #ERP #DigitalTransformation #TechConsulting #SAPCommunity #SolutionArchitecture #PricingStrategy #SAPConsulting #SAPDevelopment #SAPProjects #S4HANA #EnterpriseIT

entry.title: The Complete SAP SD Tables Reference Guide
entry.content:
1. Enterprise Structure Tables

Table Short Name Description & Usage

VKORGSales OrganizationDefines top-level selling unit. Links to Company Code (BUKRS). Controls pricing, document types.
TVKOVDistribution ChannelHow products reach customers: Direct, Wholesale, Retail. Assigned to Sales Org.
TVTASales AreaCombination of Sales Org + Distribution Channel + Division. Core SD entity.
T001WPlantManufacturing/storage location. Linked to Company Code. Used in availability check.
TVSTShipping PointPhysical dispatch point. Determined automatically based on loading group, shipping condition, plant.
T001Company CodeFI organizational unit. All SD transactions ultimately post to a Company Code.
TVSBShipping ConditionControls automatic shipping point determination. Maintained in customer master.
TVGRTransportation GroupGroups materials for route determination. Maintained in material master.
T024EPurchasing OrgUsed in inter-company and third-party sales scenarios for purchase orders.

2. Customer Master Tables (KNA1 / Business Partner)

Table Short Name Description & Usage

KNA1General DataName, address, language, country, tax number. Created once per client.
KNB1Company Code DataReconciliation account, payment terms, dunning area. One record per company code.
KNVVSales Area DataSales office, group, shipping condition, pricing procedure, customer stats group.
KNVPPartner FunctionsSP (Sold-to), SH (Ship-to), BP (Bill-to), PY (Payer). Controls document partners.
KNVSShipping DataShipping conditions, delivery priority, max partial deliveries, order combination.
KNVDCustomer Material InfoCustomer-specific material numbers, delivery plant, loading group, units of measure.
KNVKContact PersonsContact details per customer: name, department, phone, email.
KNEXLegal ControlExport/import license data for foreign trade compliance.
BUT000Business PartnerS/4HANA: Central BP master record. Replaces KNA1/LFA1. Role-based data management.
BUT100BP RolesBusiness Partner roles: FLCU00 (customer), FLVN00 (vendor). Role assignment table.

3. Material Master Tables – SD Relevant Views

Table Short Name Description & Usage

MARAGeneral DataMaterial number, material type (MTART), division, basic UoM, weight, dimensions.
MAKTMaterial DescriptionShort text per language. Used in order line items, invoices, output forms.
MVKESales Org Data 1&2Item category group (MTPOS), sales unit, minimum order qty, delivery plant, tax data.
MARCPlant Data / MRPMRP type, lot size, procurement type, availability check rule (MTVFP), lead times.
MARDStorage Location StockUnrestricted, quality inspection, blocked stock per storage location.
MLANTax ClassificationTax classification per country/tax category. Used in pricing condition MWST.
MVKGRMaterial GroupUsed for pricing access sequences and statistics grouping.
MARMUnits of MeasureAll alternative UoMs with conversion factors (e.g., EA, KG, PAL, CTN).
MBEWMaterial ValuationStandard price, moving average price. Used for COGS posting at goods issue.
MLGNWarehouse Mgmt DataWM storage type, bulk storage indicator. Required for WM picking in delivery.

4. Sales Document Tables (Order Header, Item, Schedule Lines)

Table Short Name Description & Usage

VBAKSales Order HeaderDocument number, sales area, document type, created by, date, pricing date, customer.
VBAPSales Order ItemMaterial, quantity, UoM, plant, item category, pricing data, schedule line data.
VBEPSchedule LinesConfirmed quantity, delivery date, MRP relevance, movement type, schedule line cat.
VBKDBusiness DataPayment terms, incoterms, purchase order number. Per header and item level.
VBPAPartnersAll partner functions (SP, SH, BP, PY, LS, WE, RE) for header and item.
VBFADocument FlowLinks all related documents: inquiry → quotation → order → delivery → billing. Key for tracing.
VBUKHeader StatusOverall delivery status, billing status, rejection status, credit status at header.
VBUPItem StatusDelivery status, billing status, goods movement status at item level.
VBAK-BSTNKPO ReferenceCustomer's purchase order number. Used for invoice matching and SOX compliance.
VAKPAAddress DataShip-to address override per sales order item. One-time customer address.

5. Delivery & Shipment Tables

Table Short Name Description & Usage

LIKPDelivery HeaderDelivery number, delivery type (LF/NL/LR), ship-to party, shipping point, planned GI date.
LIPSDelivery ItemMaterial, delivery qty, plant, storage location, batch, serial numbers, item category.
LIDSDelivery Item DetailPacking data, handling unit assignment, dangerous goods classification.
VTTKShipment HeaderShipment number, carrier, route, transportation planning point, forwarding agent.
VTTPShipment ItemsDeliveries included in shipment. Links LIKP to VTTK.
VEKPHandling Unit HeaderPacking material, gross weight, volume, sealing number, tracking number.
VEPOHU ItemContents of each handling unit. Links to delivery item LIPS.
NSDM_V_TCTransport ConnectionLast-mile delivery tracking data. Used with track-and-trace solutions.
EKKOPO Header (3rd Party)Purchase order created automatically for third-party (item cat TAS) scenarios.
EKPOPO Item (3rd Party)PO line item with material, qty, vendor. Linked to sales order via VBAP-AUFNR.

6. Billing & Accounting Tables

Table Short Name Description & Usage

VBRKBilling HeaderBilling document number, billing type (F2/RE/G2), payer, billing date, net value.
VBRPBilling ItemMaterial, billed qty, net price, tax amount, revenue account, cost. Links to VBAP.
VBSSSD Collect. RunBilling due list run data. VF04 collective billing processing log.
BKPFAccounting HeaderFI document created from billing. Company code, posting date, document type RV.
BSEGAccounting Line ItemsDebit/credit postings: customer AR, revenue G/L, tax G/L, freight G/L.
VBREVERevenue RecognitionRevenue distribution schedule for multi-period billing plan items.
KONARebate AgreementsRebate agreement number, validity, rebate recipient, condition type (BO01-BO05).
KONHCondition HeaderHeader data for pricing conditions. Validity dates, scales, release status.
KONVConditions (Doc)All condition values in a sales/billing document. Pricing trace source table.
KONDConditions (Master)Condition records per condition type. Accessed via VK13/VK11. Price master data.

7. Pricing & Condition Tables

Table Short Name Description & Usage

A304Material Price (PR00)Condition table for price per sales org, distribution channel, material.
A305Price List (PR00)Price list type-based pricing. Customer price list assigned in customer master.
A306Customer-MaterialCondition table for customer-specific material pricing records.
T685Condition TypesAll condition types: PR00, K007, RA01, KF00, MWST, HD00. Controls calculation.
T682Access SequencesAccess sequence tables. Defines search order for finding condition records.
T683Pricing ProceduresPricing procedure definition. Steps, conditions, from/to, requirements, alt calc.
T683SPricing Procedure Det.Pricing procedure determination: sales area + doc pricing procedure + cust PP.
T681Condition TablesAll condition table definitions. Each table defines key fields for conditions.
PRCD_ELEMENTSNew Pricing (S/4)S/4HANA 1909+: Replaces KONV. New pricing element table in universal journal.
T006Units of MeasureUoM master table. ISO codes, decimal places. Used in pricing scale unit.

8. Output Determination Tables (NACE)

Table Short Name Description & Usage

NASTMessage StatusOutput records for all SD documents. Processed/unprocessed, medium, printer, time.
T685AOutput TypesAll output types: BA00, RD00, LD00, RD03. Controls transmission medium, partner.
TNAPRProcessing ProgramsPrint program, form name (SmartForm/Adobe), routine assigned to output type.
VNASTMessage Log (Del.)Output records specifically for delivery documents.
VBDKRDoc Output (Billing)Billing output records. Invoice print, email, EDI transmission status.
T166Output Cond. TablesCondition tables for output determination (analogous to pricing cond. tables).
TOASPArchiving LinksDocument links for archived output (optical archiving / SAP DMS integration).

9. Credit Management Tables

Table Short Name Description & Usage

KNKKCredit Control DataCredit limit, credit exposure, risk category, credit control area per customer.
CNCREDITACCOUNTCredit AccountS/4HANA credit management: credit account replacing KNKK. FIN-FSCM-CR.
T014Credit Control AreasDefines credit control area master data. Assignment to company codes.
VBUK-CMGSTCredit StatusCredit hold status on sales document header (A=not checked, B=OK, C=held, D=released).
KKBERCredit Check LogLog of automatic credit checks. Timestamp, result, credit exposure at check time.
FD32Credit Master (T-Code)Transaction to maintain credit limit, risk category, payment history for customer.

10. Availability Check (ATP) & MRP Tables

Table Short Name Description & Usage

MARC-MTVFPAvail Check RuleAvailability checking rule (01, 02, CH). Controls what stocks/receipts are considered.

11. Partner Functions Tables

Table Short Name Description & Usage

VBPADocument PartnersAll partner functions per document: SP, SH, BP, PY, LS (carrier), WE (goods recip).
TPARPartner FunctionsMaster list of all partner function codes and descriptions.
TPABTPartner Procedure Det.Determination of partner functions from customer master to sales documents.
KNVPCustomer PartnersPartner functions maintained in customer master. Source for default document partners.
T180Account GroupsCustomer account groups (SOLD, SHIP, BILL, PAYR). Controls field selection in BP.

12. Customer-Material Info & Condition Records

Table Short Name Description & Usage

KNVDCustomer-Mat. InfoVD51: Customer-specific delivery and shipping data per material. Source: customer orders.
MVKEMaterial Sales ViewsItem category group (MTPOS_MARA), sales unit, min. order qty, delivery plant.
MLANTax DataTax classification per departure country and material. Used in tax condition MWST.
A502Cust/Mat ConditionsPrice conditions specific to customer-material combination. Accessed via VK13.

13. Document Types & Configuration Tables

Table Short Name Description & Usage

TVAKSales Doc TypesOR, CS, RO, SO, QT, KA, DR, CR and all custom types. Controls number ranges, screens.
TVAPItem CategoriesTAN, TANN, TAB, TAD, TAX, TATX, RENN. Controls pricing, delivery relevance, billing.
TVEPSchedule Line Cat.CP, CB, LS, BN. Controls movement type, MRP relevance, transfer of requirements.
TVLKDelivery TypesLF (outbound), NL (replenishment), LR (returns), NLCC (cross-company). Controls GI.
TVFKBilling Doc TypesF2 (invoice), RE (returns), G2 (credit), L2 (debit), F5 (proforma), IV (inter-co.).
TVLSPShipping PointsShipping point master with address, calendar, factory calendar assignment.
T173Item Cat GroupsMaterial item category groups: NORM, BANS, ERLA, LUMF, DIEN, LEIH for BOM/service.

14. Text Determination Tables

Table Short Name Description & Usage

STXHText HeaderText object, text ID, language, key. Master table for all SAP long texts.
STXLText LinesActual text content in compressed format. Linked to STXH. Use READ_TEXT to access.
TTXIDText IDsText type definitions per text object (VBBK for order header, VBBP for order item).
TTXOBText ObjectsText objects: VBBK (order), VBBP (item), VDKK (delivery), VBRK (billing).

15. Rebates & Settlement Tables

Table Short Name Description & Usage

KONARebate AgreementsRebate agreement: validity, recipient, accrual rate, settlement calendar.
KNUMA_PICondition No (Reb)Rebate condition record number. Links rebate agreement to condition type.
VBOFRebate BasisBilling documents forming the basis for rebate accruals. Updated with each invoice.
VBOXRebate BillingSettlement billing documents (credit memos) for final rebate settlement.

Pro Tips for Using SAP SD Tables

  • Use SE16N to directly query any table with filters — faster than SE16 for large tables.
  • VBFA (Document Flow) is the most powerful tracing tool — enter any SD document number to see the complete chain.
  • KONV stores runtime pricing conditions from documents; KONH/KOND store master condition records.
  • In S/4HANA, BSEG is replaced by ACDOCA (Universal Journal) — always check the migration note for your release.
  • Use ST05 (SQL Trace) to discover which tables are being read during a transaction — great for performance tuning.
  • PRCD_ELEMENTS replaces KONV in S/4HANA 1909+. For older systems, KONV is your pricing debug table.
  • Always combine VBAK + VBAP + VBEP for complete sales order data — never query just one of them alone.

Found this useful? Bookmark it for your next SD debugging session, or drop a comment below with tables you'd add.

entry.title: Beyond Configuration: Where SAP SD-FICO Integration Still Falls Short (and What to Do About It)
entry.content:
Beyond Configuration: Where SAP SD-FICO Integration Still Falls Short (and What to Do About It)

Research Study on SAP SD-FICO Integration Optimization

Author: Satyendra Mittapalli Title: SAP SD Lead Consultant | S/4HANA Order-to-Cash & Pricing Specialist Credentials: 14+ years hands-on SAP SD/FICO integration experience across enterprise implementations

Date: May 2026

Keywords: SAP SD-FICO Integration, Enterprise Resource Planning, Revenue Recogni-tion Automation, Data Quality Management, Industry-Specific Customization, Emerging Technologies Integration, Financial Compliance, Order-to-Cash Optimization

 

Table of Contents

  1. Executive summary
  2. Introduction
  3. Authors project experience & implementation context
  4. Current state of research
  5. Identified research gaps 5.1 Real-Time Revenue Recognition Automation Using AI/ML 5.2 Cross-Module Data Quality and Reconciliation Frameworks 5.3 Dynamic Pricing Integration and Real-Time Account Determination 5.4 Post-Implementation Performance Measurement and Benefit Realization 5.5 Industry-Specific Integra-tion Customization Frameworks 5.6 Integration of Emerging Technologies (IoT, Blockchain) with SD-FICO
  6. Priority areas & strategic recommendations
  7. Conclusion
  8. Author information & professional background
  9. References

 

Executive Summary

SAP Sales and Distribution (SD) and Financial Accounting and Controlling (FICO) in-tegration represents a mature and well-established operational capability within enter-prise resource planning (ERP) implementations. However, despite decades of practical deployment and significant organizational investment, critical research gaps persist that limit organizations' ability to achieve optimal financial accuracy, operational eﬀiciency, and strategic value from these integrated systems.

This research identifies six strategic research gaps and proposes three high-priority areas for investigation and development. Through analysis of 14+ years of hands-on implementation experience and synthesis of peer-reviewed literature, we demonstrate that while foundational SD-FICO integration knowledge is well-documented, emerging challenges—particularly those related to real-time revenue recognition automation, cross-module data quality assurance, and industry-specific customization—remain substantially underexplored in academic research.

Key Findings:

  • AI/ML-Driven Revenue Recognition Automation represents the highest-impact opportunity, with potential to reduce manual reconciliation effort by 40-60% and accelerate financial close cycles by 3-5 business days.
  • Data Quality and Continuous Reconciliation frameworks remain fragmented; 38% of organizations cite poor data quality as a primary barrier to ERP integration suc-cess.
  • Industry-Specific Integration Customization is underdeveloped; organizations lack standardized playbooks for CPG, medical devices, SaaS, and other specialized sectors, increasing implementation costs by 30-40%.
  • Post-Implementation Performance Measurement is inadequately addressed in lit-erature; 68% of organizations fail to achieve projected ROI within 18 months, yet few studies track SD-FICO integration maturity longitudinally.
  • Dynamic Pricing Integration with financial controls lacks SAP-specific guidance, creating compliance risks and revenue leakage of 3-8% in organizations with com-plex pricing models.
  • Emerging Technologies (IoT, Blockchain) integration with SD-FICO remains largely theoretical; no empirical studies validate practical frameworks for connect-ing operational event streams to accounting logic.

This research provides actionable frameworks, implementation considerations, and evidence-based recommendations for practitioners, academics, and technology vendors seeking to advance SD-FICO integration capabilities.

 

Introduction

Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems have fundamentally transformed financial and operational management within organizations worldwide. Among the most critical integration points within these systems are Sales and Distribution (SD) and Financial Ac-counting and Controlling (FICO)—modules that together govern the complete Order-to-Cash (O2C) process, from customer order entry through revenue recognition and cash collection.

Despite the maturity of SD-FICO integration as a practice domain, organizations continue to face substantial challenges that delay financial reporting, compromise audit readiness, create revenue recognition risks, and impede strategic decision-making. These challenges are not primarily attributable to configuration gaps in existing implementations; rather, they emerge from systemic limitations in how organizations approach real-time data val-idation, regulatory compliance automation, industry-specific customization, and integra-tion with emerging technologies.

Purpose and Scope:

This research systematically identifies and analyzes six critical research gaps in SAP SD-FICO integration, grounded in both academic literature review and practical implemen-tation experience spanning 14+ years and multiple enterprise environments. The study proposes actionable research directions, identifies implementation frameworks, and pri-oritizes areas for future investigation based on impact potential and technical feasibility.

Research Questions Addressed:

  • What specific capabilities and frameworks are absent from current SD-FICO integra-tion practice and literature?
  • How can machine learning and AI enhance revenue recognition accuracy and finan-cial close cycle times?
  • What mechanisms would enable continuous, real-time data quality assurance across SD-FICO flows?
  • How should SD-FICO integration be customized for distinct industry verticals (CPG, medical devices, SaaS, manufacturing)?
  • How can organizations measure and sustain SD-FICO integration benefits over ex-tended post-implementation periods?
  • What architectural approaches enable IoT and blockchain technologies to enhance SD-FICO audit readiness and operational transparency?

Document Structure:

This research paper is organized as follows: Section 2 presents the author's implementa-tion context and background. Section 3 summarizes the current state of SD-FICO research and practice. Sections 4 and 5 detail six identified research gaps with supporting evidence, implementation considerations, and suggested research directions. Section 6 prioritizes

 

the most impactful gaps for near-term investigation. Section 7 concludes with implica-tions for practitioners, researchers, and technology vendors.

 

Author's Project Experience & Implementation Context

This research is grounded in 14+ years of hands-on SAP SD/FICO integration experience across enterprise implementations. As an SAP SD Lead Consultant at Wipro, the author has led end-to-end Order-to-Cash transformations across multiple industries—including medical devices (embecta/BD), food manufacturing (Perdue Farms, Tate & Lyle), phar-maceuticals, publishing, and high-volume divestitures (PerkinElmer Analytics).

Key Implementation Areas:

  • Revenue Account Determination & SD-FI Integration: Configured VKOA account determination for greenfield S/4HANA environments, mapped SD billing to FICO GL accounts, and resolved recurring revenue posting failures during financial audits—directly experiencing the data quality and automation gaps identified in this research.
  • Complex Pricing & Billing Scenarios: Designed and validated commodity-linked pricing formulas (Perdue Farms), multi-currency pricing procedures, tax-compliant configurations (Vertex integration across multiple US jurisdictions), and handled trade promotion accounting flows typical in CPG environments.
  • EDI/IDoc Integration & Data Quality: Built end-to-end EDI 810 invoice workflows with external logistics partners, managed IDoc monitoring and troubleshooting, and implemented data migration using LSMW/LTMC—revealing persistent data syn-chronization challenges between SD and FICO that motivate the cross-module rec-onciliation research gap.
  • Industry -Specific Customization: Implemented SD configuration for medical device carve-outs (greenfield, multi-region, regulatory compliance), managed consignment processes (EMEA/APAC), and rolled out geographically distributed sales organizations during divestitures—providing direct perspective on why industry-specific frameworks are essential.
  • P ost-Go-Live Support & Optimization: Provided L2/L3 hypercare support, resolved billing-revenue mismatches post-go-live, automated test cycles using WorkSoft, and managed continuous improvement activities—observing firsthand how SD-FICO integration degrades over time without active optimization.

This section grounds the research gaps in real implementation challenges encountered across diverse organizational contexts, lending empirical credibility to the identified re-search priorities.

 

Current State of Research

SAP SD (Sales and Distribution) and FICO (Financial Accounting and Controlling) integration is a well-established and mature area of practice within enterprise resource planning implementations. Research demonstrates that integration occurs through mul-tiple critical touchpoints: goods receipt and issue transactions [1], revenue recognition and billing processes [2], and inventory valuation mechanisms [3]. Organizations have achieved significant standardization around key configuration transactions (particularly VKOA for account determination) and best practices for master data synchronization [4]. The Order-to-Cash (O2C) process represents a fully understood end-to-end work-flow with documented automation capabilities across sales order processing, delivery management, invoicing, and payment collection [5].

 

Identified Research Gaps

1.   Real-Time Revenue Recognition Automation Using AI/ML

Gap Description: While academic literature extensively covers manual revenue recogni-tion procedures and ASC 606/IFRS 15 compliance frameworks, there is a significant ab-sence of research on integrating AI-driven reconciliation agents and machine learning al-gorithms specifically within SAP SD-FICO architectures for real-time revenue recognition automation. Current research on AI in accounting [6] focuses on standalone reconciliation systems rather than how intelligent automation can be embedded into SAP's native rev-enue determination processes (VKOA, pricing procedures, and billing documents). No studies examine how machine learning models can learn from historical billing-to-revenue posting patterns to automatically optimize account determination rules or detect billing-revenue mismatches in real-time.

Why This Matters: Organizations implementing SAP S/4HANA struggle with complex multi-currency scenarios, variable consideration arrangements, and contract performance obligation interpretation—areas where AI could dramatically reduce manual intervention. The research by Khemka & Agarwal [6] demonstrates AI's potential but lacks domain-specific integration with SAP's billing and revenue modules. This gap is particularly acute for subscription-based and usage-driven business models [7], where event-driven revenue recognition requires continuous reconciliation between order-to-cash streams and finan-cial postings.

From the author's implementation perspective, medical device carve-outs (such as em-becta during its spin-off from BD) exemplify this challenge: multiple revenue recognition scenarios (point-in-time for device sales, over-time for service contracts, variable consid-eration for performance obligations) required manual monthly reconciliation between SD billing documents and FICO revenue postings, with discrepancies discovered weeks af-ter they occurred. In commodity-linked pricing environments (Perdue Farms), dynamic pricing formulas triggered during order processing required real-time validation against contract terms and ASC 606 thresholds before posting to FICO—yet the system lacked automated controls, forcing finance teams to retrospectively audit pricing-to-revenue ac-

 

curacy.

Recent studies on AI-driven automation in cloud ERP systems reveal that period-closing operations remain one of the most resource-intensive financial processes [8], creating bot-tlenecks that delay financial insights and extend close cycles by 5-10 business days. For publicly traded companies and large enterprises, extended financial close timelines di-rectly impact investor confidence, regulatory reporting schedules, and strategic decision-making velocity. In subscription-based industries specifically, revenue leakage stemming from billing-revenue misalignment typically ranges from 2-8% of reported revenue [7], representing hundreds of millions in potential annual losses for mid-market and enter-prise organizations. The current absence of embedded AI logic within SAP's revenue post-ing layer forces organizations to rely on retrospective monthly reconciliations, meaning errors are discovered weeks after they occur, complicating remediation and audit trails.

Suggested Research Direction: Develop a framework for embedding machine learning agents within SAP FICO's revenue posting logic to automatically classify complex revenue scenarios, validate contract interpretations against billing documentation, and generate pre-audited revenue recognition entries. Research should compare automated vs. manual approaches across diverse industries (SaaS, manufacturing, CPG) and measure impact on financial close cycle times, audit readiness, and compliance accuracy.

Implementation considerations should address:

  • Technical Architecture: Design event-driven microservices that integrate with SAP's messaging infrastructure (using Apache Kafka or SAP Event Stream Pro-cessor) to capture real-time billing events, apply ML-based classification models, and trigger automated FICO postings through RFC calls or OData APIs without manual intervention.  Based on the author's IDoc/EDI configuration experience, this architecture should leverage SAP's native ALE/EDI infrastructure to capture billing document creation events (VBRK/VBRP), avoiding custom development where standard mechanisms exist.  Integration with FICO posting should use RFC-triggered function modules (e.g., BAPIACCDOCUMENT_POST) rather than direct GL table updates, ensuring audit compliance and transactional integrity.
  • Data Engineering & Model Development: Establish data pipelines that normalize billing documents, contract metadata, and historical posting patterns to train super-vised learning models (Random Forest, Gradient Boosting) capable of predicting correct revenue account determination with >95% confidence. Include explainabil-ity layers (SHAP or LIME) to ensure auditors can understand model decisions.
  • Change Management & Governance: Implement phased rollout beginning with high-frequency, low-risk transactions (standard product subscriptions) before ex-panding to complex scenarios. Establish a human-in-the-loop mechanism where ML recommendations above a confidence threshold (e.g., <98%) or unusual scenarios are reviewed by finance teams before posting. Create governance frameworks that define model retraining frequency (quarterly recommended) and performance mon-itoring KPIs (accuracy, false positive rate, time-to-posting). Following best practices from the author's multi-region rollouts (PerkinElmer 24 sales organizations, Tate &

 

Lyle EMEA/APAC/Americas), establish a pilot environment representing a single business unit or geography, validate against historical transactions, and implement structured knowledge transfer with finance stakeholders before enterprise rollout. Document all configuration changes and model logic in a centralized change log to support audit evidence collection.
